title: Week 08 - Array Statistics and Extremes
description: Practice array sums, array counts, maximum values, minimum values, position tracking, and second-largest values.

questions.title: Calculate the Total Score of a Class Score Sheet

description.background: The teacher stores the scores of n students in the array scores. Now the total class score needs to be calculated.
description.task: Traverse scores and calculate the sum of all scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.hint: You can add the score at each position into a total-score variable.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        sum = sum + scores[i]
    end

    return sum
end

questions.title: Count How Many Scores in a Score Sheet Are Passing

description.background: A score of 60 or above is passing. The teacher wants to know how many scores in the score sheet are passing.
description.task: Count the number of passing scores in scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the number of passing students.
description.hint: When traversing the array, check only whether the current score reaches the line.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 60 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Calculate the Average Reward in a Daily Coin Array

description.background: The number of coins a player earns each day is stored in the array coins. The system needs to calculate how many coins are earned per day on average.
description.task: Calculate the total number of coins and the average reward, then return the average.
description.input: One array coins and one number n.
description.hint: To calculate an average, get the sum first, then divide by the number of data items.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, n)
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        sum = sum + coins[i]
    end

    return sum / n
end

questions.title: Find the Best Athlete in a Long Jump Competition

description.background: In a long jump competition, each athlete's result is stored in the array jumps. A larger result means a better rank.
description.task: Find the highest jump result and the matching athlete position.
description.input: One array jumps and one number n.
description.output: Return the highest result and the athlete position in order.
description.hint: You can first assume the first data item is the best result, then compare each item.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(jumps, n)
    local maxValue = jumps[1]
    local maxIndex = 1

    for i = 2, n do
        if jumps[i] > maxValue then
            maxValue = jumps[i]
            maxIndex = i
        end
    end

    return maxValue, maxIndex
end

questions.title: Find the Lowest Score and Its Position in a Score Sheet

description.background: The teacher wants to find the lowest score in the score sheet and know which position contains that score.
description.task: Return the lowest score in scores and its position.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the lowest score and its position in order.
description.hint: The idea is similar to maximum value search, but the comparison direction is different.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local minValue = scores[1]
    local minIndex = 1

    for i = 2, n do
        if scores[i] < minValue then
            minValue = scores[i]
            minIndex = i
        end
    end

    return minValue, minIndex
end

questions.title: Count Game Scores Above a Target Line and Sum Them

description.background: After a game competition, the system needs to count how many players scored above target and calculate the total score of those players.
description.task: Traverse scores, count scores above the target line, and sum those scores.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the count above the target line and the total score in order.
description.hint: After filtering the data that meets the condition, you can count and accumulate at the same time.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, target)
    local count = 0
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] > target then
            count = count + 1
            sum = sum + scores[i]
        end
    end

    return count, sum
end

questions.title: Find the First and Second Place Scores in a Ranking

description.background: The score ranking has not been sorted yet, and all scores are stored in the array scores. The teacher wants to directly find the highest score and second-highest score.
description.task: Traverse the array and find the first-place and second-place scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n. n is guaranteed to be at least 2.
description.output: Return the highest score and second-highest score in order.
description.hint: When comparing a new score, consider that it may affect first place or only affect second place.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local first = scores[1]
    local second = scores[2]

    if second > first then
        local temp = first
        first = second
        second = temp
    end

    for i = 3, n do
        if scores[i] > first then
            second = first
            first = scores[i]
        elseif scores[i] > second then
            second = scores[i]
        end
    end

    return first, second
end

questions.title: Find the Difference Between First and Second Place in Long Jump

description.background: After a long jump competition, the teacher wants to know how many centimeters farther first place jumped than second place.
description.task: Find the highest result and second-highest result, then return their difference.
description.input: One array jumps and one number n. n is guaranteed to be at least 2.
description.output: Return the difference between first-place and second-place results.
description.hint: Maintain the two best results first, then calculate the difference.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(jumps, n)
    local first = jumps[1]
    local second = jumps[2]

    if second > first then
        local temp = first
        first = second
        second = temp
    end

    for i = 3, n do
        if jumps[i] > first then
            second = first
            first = jumps[i]
        elseif jumps[i] > second then
            second = jumps[i]
        end
    end

    return first - second
end

questions.title: Count Score Ranges and Find the Highest Score Position

description.background: The teacher wants to do a comprehensive analysis of a score sheet. Scores of 90 or above are excellent, scores of 60 or above but below 90 are passing, and scores below 60 need improvement. The teacher also wants to know the position of the highest score.
description.task: Count excellent, passing, and needs-improvement students, and find the highest score position.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the excellent count, passing count, needs-improvement count, and highest-score position in order.
description.hint: One traversal can complete both range counting and maximum-position updates.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local excellent = 0
    local pass = 0
    local needImprove = 0
    local maxValue = scores[1]
    local maxIndex = 1

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 90 then
            excellent = excellent + 1
        elseif scores[i] >= 60 then
            pass = pass + 1
        else
            needImprove = needImprove + 1
        end

        if scores[i] > maxValue then
            maxValue = scores[i]
            maxIndex = i
        end
    end

    return excellent, pass, needImprove, maxIndex
end
